summary: Implementation plan for the 1985-NY-Yankees Azure Function: a nightly Timer-triggered Python 3.11 function that queries GPT-4o via TRAPI, validates the returned roster, and persists it to a dedicated Azure Blob Storage account using Managed Identity. Covers Bicep IaC, CI/CD with staging-slot swap, observability, security hardening, and full test coverage.
assumptions: TRAPI supports Azure AD Managed Identity bearer token authentication; if not, a Key Vault-stored credential will be used.
TRAPI endpoint URL and API version will be provided by the TRAPI team before APP-01 begins.
The roster scope is limited to the 25-man active roster (24–28 range accounts for minor variations in historical records).
TRAPI is reachable over the public internet; no VNet integration required for initial deployment.
Consumption Plan is acceptable; no strict SLA on the 2 AM window (Premium Plan not required for MVP).
GitHub Actions runners have OIDC federation with Azure for passwordless deployments.
Existing Bicep modules (storage, functionapp, rbac, monitoring) in infra/ will be extended rather than replaced.
Application Insights sampling rate will be set to 10% to control ingestion costs.
Staging slot is available on the Consumption Plan (requires Standard or above for slots — will use a separate staging Function App if slots are unavailable on Consumption).
Key Vault zone-redundancy (ZRS SKU) is available in the target Azure region.
open_questions: Does TRAPI support Azure AD Managed Identity bearer tokens? If not, what credential type is required?
What is the TRAPI base URL and API version for the GPT-4o endpoint?
Should the roster include only the 25-man active roster, or also coaching staff and IL players? This affects prompt template wording and validation thresholds.
Is TRAPI accessible over the public internet, or does it require VNet integration and private endpoint for storage?
Is there a strict SLA on the 2 AM nightly execution? If yes, Premium Plan with always-ready instances must replace Consumption Plan.
What is the specific GPT-4o model version to pin in the prompt template to ensure deterministic output?
Is a staging deployment slot available on the selected Consumption Plan, or should a separate staging Function App be provisioned?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Resolve all blocking decisions, provision baseline Azure infrastructure, and establish CI/CD pipeline scaffolding so that application development can proceed in parallel.
entry_criteria: Azure subscription and resource group available
GitHub repository access confirmed for rciapala
TRAPI team contact identified for auth/endpoint questions
exit_criteria: TRAPI auth mechanism and endpoint confirmed (or decision to use Key Vault credential documented)
Bicep IaC provisions Function App, dedicated Storage Account, Key Vault, App Insights, and all RBAC assignments
GitHub Actions CI workflow runs and passes on main branch
Local development environment documented and reproducible

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement and unit-test all application logic: TRAPI client, response validation, blob writer, timer trigger, and prompt template.
entry_criteria: TRAPI endpoint and auth scope confirmed
exit_criteria: All functional requirements implemented (TRAPI query, schema validation, blob write, timer trigger)
Unit test suite passing with >80% coverage on core modules
Prompt template pinned to specific GPT-4o model version in source control
Failed-response path (failed/ prefix) implemented and tested

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Harden reliability, security, and observability: retry logic, idempotency, structured logging, custom metrics, alerting, and Key Vault integration.
entry_criteria: Core Build wave exit criteria met
exit_criteria: Exponential-backoff retry (3 retries, 45s timeout) implemented and tested
Conditional PUT (If-None-Match) preventing duplicate blob writes verified
All three alert rules active in Application Insights (failure, duration, data quality)
Structured log events emitted for all key function lifecycle points
No hardcoded secrets; all credentials sourced from Managed Identity or Key Vault references
Security scan passes with no critical findings

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Validate end-to-end in staging, complete integration tests, deploy to production via slot swap, and confirm monitoring is live.
entry_criteria: Staging environment fully deployed and accessible
exit_criteria: End-to-end integration test passing in staging (known players Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson present in blob output)
Production deployment completed via GitHub Actions staging-slot swap
All monitoring and alerting confirmed active in production
All success criteria in spec.md checked off

tasks.title: Resolve TRAPI auth mechanism and endpoint details
tasks.description: Confirm with the TRAPI team whether Managed Identity bearer tokens are supported, obtain the base URL and API version, and determine the required OAuth scope. Document the decision in spec.md under Open Questions 1 and 2. If Managed Identity is unsupported, update spec.md to specify the Key Vault secret name and rotation policy for the TRAPI credential. Update the TRAPI auth section in spec.md with: confirmed endpoint URL, API version, OAuth scope or Key Vault secret reference, and whether a fallback credential type is needed.
outputs: Updated spec.md with confirmed TRAPI auth mechanism, endpoint, API version, and OAuth scope
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI endpoint URL and API version documented in spec.md
Auth mechanism confirmed (Managed Identity scope or Key Vault secret name)
Open Questions 1 and 2 in spec.md marked as resolved
If Key Vault credential required, secret name and rotation schedule defined
risks: TRAPI may not support Managed Identity, requiring Key Vault credential and additional security controls
Endpoint details unavailable before development starts, blocking APP-01

tasks.title: Confirm roster scope and validate player count thresholds
tasks.description: Resolve Open Question 3: define whether the roster includes only the 25-man active roster or also coaches, IL players, and front-office staff. Update spec.md validation thresholds (currently 24–28) and prompt template wording accordingly. Document the decision so the prompt author and QA team can align. Update spec.md Requirement 1 validation thresholds and the prompt template section to reflect the agreed roster scope.
outputs: Updated spec.md with confirmed roster scope, validated player count range, and updated prompt template guidance
acceptance_criteria: Roster scope explicitly defined (active roster only vs. expanded)
Player count validation thresholds updated in spec.md
Prompt template wording updated to match scope decision
Open Question 3 in spec.md marked as resolved
risks: Ambiguous scope may cause validation failures if GPT-4o returns coaching staff
Threshold mismatch could trigger false-positive failure alerts in production

tasks.title: Provision core Azure infrastructure via Bicep
tasks.description: Extend or author Bicep modules to provision: Function App (Consumption, Python 3.11, system-assigned MI), dedicated Storage Account (Standard_LRS, Cool tier, soft-delete 7 days, container yankees-roster private), Azure Key Vault (zone-redundant, Key Vault Secrets User for MI), Application Insights (workspace-based, 30-day retention, sampling), and all RBAC assignments (Storage Blob Data Contributor scoped to container, Key Vault Secrets User). Host storage account must be separate from application data storage account.
inputs: spec.md Architecture and Resource Requirements sections
acceptance_criteria: Bicep deploys without errors to a dev resource group
Function App has system-assigned Managed Identity enabled
Dedicated storage account created with Standard_LRS, Cool tier, soft-delete 7 days
Container yankees-roster created with no public/anonymous access
Host storage account is distinct from application data storage account
Key Vault provisioned with zone-redundancy and correct RBAC
RBAC: Storage Blob Data Contributor scoped to yankees-roster container only
RBAC: Key Vault Secrets User assigned to Function App MI
Application Insights connected to Function App via APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING app setting
bicep what-if shows no unintended resource deletions
risks: Consumption Plan may not support deployment slots; staging environment may require a separate Function App
TRAPI auth decision (DES-01) may require additional Key Vault secrets or scope configurations

tasks.title: Provision Application Insights alerting rules
tasks.description: Author infra/modules/alerts.bicep to provision all three alert rules: execution failure count > 0 in 1-hour window, function duration > 90 seconds, and custom metric player_count_returned < 24 or > 40. Wire action group to notify rciapala via email. All alerts scoped to the Function App resource.
outputs: infra/modules/alerts.bicep (updated/created with all 3 alert rules and email action group)
acceptance_criteria: Three alert rules provisioned: failure count, duration, and data quality (player_count_returned)
Email action group notifies rciapala
Alerts scoped to the correct Function App resource
Bicep deploys without errors
Test alert fires correctly when manually triggered
risks: Custom metric player_count_returned must be emitted by the app before the data quality alert can be validated

tasks.title: Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline with staging-slot swap
tasks.description: Create GitHub Actions workflows: a CI workflow (lint, unit tests, coverage gate) triggered on pull requests, and a CD workflow triggered on push to main that deploys to a staging slot (or staging Function App if slots unavailable on Consumption), runs a smoke test, then swaps to production. Use OIDC federation for passwordless Azure authentication. Parameterize slot vs. separate-app strategy based on INF-01 findings.
acceptance_criteria: CI workflow runs lint and pytest on every PR
CD workflow deploys to staging on push to main
Smoke test validates function is registered and health endpoint responds before slot swap
Slot swap (or staging-to-prod promotion) completes without downtime
OIDC federation used; no long-lived credentials in GitHub secrets
Pipeline fails and blocks swap if smoke test fails
risks: Consumption Plan does not support deployment slots natively; may require a separate staging Function App increasing cost and complexity
OIDC federation setup requires Azure AD app registration with federated credential

tasks.title: Implement TRAPI client with retry and timeout
tasks.description: Implement src/trapi_client.py: authenticate via DefaultAzureCredential to obtain a bearer token for the confirmed TRAPI scope, send the pinned GPT-4o prompt via HTTP POST with a 45-second timeout, and implement 3-retry exponential-backoff on transient failures (429, 5xx). Log trapi_request_sent (model version, prompt hash) and trapi_response_received (token count, latency ms, player count) structured events.
inputs: DES-01 output (TRAPI endpoint, auth scope, API version)
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI client authenticates via DefaultAzureCredential with confirmed OAuth scope
HTTP POST timeout set to 45 seconds
3 retries with exponential backoff on 429 and 5xx responses
trapi_request_sent log event includes model version and prompt hash
trapi_response_received log event includes token count, latency ms, and player count
Unit tests mock TRAPI HTTP responses for success, timeout, and retry scenarios
No hardcoded credentials or endpoint URLs (all from environment/app settings)
risks: Blocked until DES-01 confirms TRAPI endpoint and auth scope
GPT-4o response format may vary across model versions; pinning is critical

tasks.title: Author and pin prompt template for 1985 Yankees roster
tasks.description: Create prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt with a precise, unambiguous prompt requesting the 1985 New York Yankees roster in the specified JSON schema. Pin the GPT-4o model version in configuration (not hardcoded in prompt). Scope prompt to confirmed roster definition from DES-02 (active roster only or expanded). Include output format instructions to produce valid JSON matching the expected schema.
inputs: DES-02 output (confirmed roster scope and player count thresholds)
acceptance_criteria: Prompt stored in prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt in source control
Prompt explicitly requests JSON output matching the expected schema
GPT-4o model version pinned via app setting (not hardcoded in prompt file)
Prompt wording aligned with confirmed roster scope from DES-02
Known players (Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson) consistently returned in manual test
risks: GPT-4o may return variable results even with a pinned model version; prompt engineering iteration may be needed
Roster scope ambiguity (DES-02) directly affects prompt wording and validation thresholds

tasks.title: Implement response schema validation and failure routing
tasks.description: Implement src/roster_validator.py: validate TRAPI response against the expected JSON schema (players array with name, position, jersey_number fields) and confirm player count is within the confirmed range from DES-02. On validation failure, route the raw response to the failed/ blob prefix path and emit a structured log event. Do not write to the primary output path on failure.
acceptance_criteria: Validates response JSON against schema: players array, each with name (string), position (string), jersey_number (integer)
Validates player count within confirmed range (e.g., 24–28)
Returns a typed result (success/failure) with the parsed roster or error detail
On failure, function routes to failed/ prefix write (not primary output path)
Unit tests cover: valid response, schema mismatch, player count out of range, malformed JSON
risks: Threshold boundaries (24–28) may generate false positives if GPT-4o returns coaches; depends on DES-02 resolution

tasks.title: Implement blob writer with conditional PUT and Managed Identity
tasks.description: Implement src/blob_writer.py: write validated roster JSON to yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json in the dedicated storage account using DefaultAzureCredential (Managed Identity). Use If-None-Match: * conditional PUT to prevent duplicate writes on same-day retrigger. Implement a separate write path for failed/ prefix blobs. Log blob_write_succeeded with blob URI on success.
inputs: INF-01 output (storage account name, container name)
acceptance_criteria: Writes to yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json using UTC date
Uses DefaultAzureCredential (no connection strings or account keys)
Conditional PUT with If-None-Match: * header prevents duplicate writes
On 412 Precondition Failed (blob already exists), logs a warning and exits gracefully without error
Failed/ prefix write path implemented for validation failures
Unit tests mock BlobServiceClient for success, duplicate-write (412), and auth failure scenarios
blob_write_succeeded log event includes full blob URI
risks: Storage Blob Data Contributor RBAC must be scoped to the container (not account) per spec; misconfigured RBAC will cause 403 errors

tasks.title: Implement Timer Trigger function entry point
tasks.description: Implement the Azure Functions v2 Timer Trigger function GetAndStoreYankeesRoster in function_app.py. Configure schedule 0 0 2 * * * with useMonitor: true and run_on_startup: false. Wire together trapi_client, roster_validator, and blob_writer. Emit structured log events: function_started and function_completed. Set functionTimeout to 120 seconds and maxConcurrentCalls to 1 in host.json.
acceptance_criteria: Timer Trigger schedule set to 0 0 2 * * * (UTC)
useMonitor: true set in trigger configuration
run_on_startup: false to prevent accidental execution on deploy
host.json: functionTimeout set to 00:02:00 (120 seconds)
host.json: extensions.queues.maxConcurrentCalls or singleton lock ensures single-instance execution
function_started and function_completed log events emitted on every execution
Unit test verifies the function wires all components correctly with mocked dependencies
risks: Timer Trigger useMonitor requires a storage account for singleton lease; must be the host storage account, not the data storage account

tasks.title: Implement Key Vault references and secret externalization
tasks.description: Configure App Settings to use Key Vault references (@Microsoft.KeyVault(...)) for any TRAPI credentials not sourced via Managed Identity. Ensure no secrets appear in local.settings.json, application code, or source control. Audit all environment variable reads in the codebase. Update local.settings.json.example with placeholder values only.
acceptance_criteria: All TRAPI credentials stored in Key Vault; App Settings use @Microsoft.KeyVault() references
No secrets, connection strings, or API keys present in source code or committed files
local.settings.json.example contains only placeholder values
local.settings.json is in .gitignore
Function App MI has Key Vault Secrets User role confirmed in deployed environment
Static code scan finds no hardcoded credentials
risks: Key Vault reference resolution requires the Function App MI to have Secrets User role before first deployment; ordering matters in Bicep

tasks.title: Implement structured logging and custom metrics via OpenTelemetry
tasks.description: Instrument all key log events (function_started, trapi_request_sent, trapi_response_received, blob_write_succeeded, function_completed) using the OpenTelemetry SDK with structured key-value properties. Emit the player_count_returned custom metric on each successful run. Configure Application Insights sampling in host.json to control ingestion costs.
outputs: requirements.txt (updated with opentelemetry deps if needed)
acceptance_criteria: All five key log events emitted with structured properties on every execution
trapi_request_sent includes model_version and prompt_hash properties
trapi_response_received includes token_count, latency_ms, and player_count properties
blob_write_succeeded includes blob_uri property
player_count_returned custom metric emitted to Application Insights on success
Application Insights sampling enabled in host.json
Log events visible in Application Insights Live Metrics and Logs blade
risks: OpenTelemetry SDK version compatibility with azure-functions SDK must be verified
Custom metric player_count_returned must be emitted before INF-02 data quality alert can be validated

tasks.title: Implement exponential-backoff retry and idempotency hardening
tasks.description: Harden the TRAPI client retry logic: implement true exponential backoff (e.g., 1s, 2s, 4s) with jitter on 429 and 5xx responses, cap at 3 retries. Verify the conditional PUT (If-None-Match) in blob_writer.py handles the 412 Precondition Failed response gracefully as a no-op (not an error). Add unit tests for retry exhaustion path.
acceptance_criteria: Retry delays follow exponential backoff with jitter (e.g., 1s, 2s, 4s base)
Maximum 3 retries before raising final exception
429 responses trigger retry with Retry-After header respected if present
412 Precondition Failed on blob write logged as warning, not exception
Unit tests cover: retry exhaustion, 412 no-op, successful retry on 2nd attempt
risks: Retry with exponential backoff could cause total execution time to approach the 120-second timeout if TRAPI is consistently slow

tasks.title: Write unit test suite for core modules
tasks.description: Write comprehensive unit tests for trapi_client.py, roster_validator.py, blob_writer.py, and function_app.py. Use pytest with mocks for all external dependencies (Azure SDK, TRAPI HTTP). Target >80% line coverage. Include parameterized tests for known players (Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson) and edge cases (boundary player counts, malformed JSON, auth failures).
acceptance_criteria: pytest runs all tests without errors
Line coverage >80% on src/ modules
Known player assertions: Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson present in parsed response
Edge cases covered: player count at boundaries (23, 24, 28, 29), malformed JSON, HTTP timeout, auth failure
All tests are isolated with no real HTTP calls or Azure SDK calls
Coverage report generated via pytest-cov
risks: Mocking DefaultAzureCredential correctly requires care to avoid masking real auth bugs

tasks.title: Run end-to-end integration test in staging environment
tasks.description: Manually trigger the function in the staging environment and verify: TRAPI is called successfully, the response is validated, the blob is written to yankees-roster/{date}.json, known players (Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson) are present in the blob, and a duplicate trigger produces a no-op (412 handled). Verify all alert rules fire correctly in Application Insights.
outputs: tests/integration/test_e2e_staging.py or manual test report in results/
acceptance_criteria: Function executes successfully in staging without errors
Blob yankees-roster/{today_utc}.json exists in dedicated storage account after trigger
Blob contains players array with Mattingly, Winfield, and Henderson
Player count within confirmed valid range
Duplicate trigger on same day writes nothing (412 no-op confirmed in logs)
Structured log events visible in Application Insights for all 5 key events
player_count_returned custom metric visible in Application Insights Metrics
Failure scenario test: inject invalid TRAPI response, verify blob written to failed/ prefix
risks: Staging environment TRAPI access must be granted separately from production
Timer Trigger cannot be manually invoked via portal on Consumption Plan; use Azure Functions Core Tools or HTTP admin endpoint

tasks.title: Deploy to production via GitHub Actions slot swap
tasks.description: Execute the CD pipeline to deploy the validated build to production via the staging-slot swap (or staging-to-prod promotion). Confirm the function is registered, the timer trigger is active, and the first nightly execution succeeds. Verify all monitoring and alerting is live in the production Application Insights workspace.
outputs: Production deployment record in GitHub Actions
Production Function App with active Timer Trigger
acceptance_criteria: CD pipeline completes without errors
Function App in production shows GetAndStoreYankeesRoster registered with correct schedule
First nightly execution at 2:00 AM UTC completes successfully
Blob written to production storage account after first execution
All three Application Insights alert rules active in production
No secrets visible in application settings (Key Vault references in use)
Rollback plan documented and tested (re-swap slot or redeploy previous artifact)
risks: Production TRAPI endpoint may differ from staging; confirm app settings before swap
First production execution is at 2 AM UTC; monitoring must be active before that window

tasks.title: Write README with local dev setup, TRAPI auth, and blob naming
tasks.description: Write README.md covering: local development setup (Python 3.11, Azure Functions Core Tools, local.settings.json configuration), TRAPI authentication instructions (how to obtain a token locally), blob naming convention and how to inspect blobs, and how to run unit tests. Include architecture diagram or ASCII overview of the data flow.
acceptance_criteria: README covers local dev setup end-to-end (clone, install deps, configure settings, run locally)
TRAPI auth instructions describe how to obtain a bearer token for local testing
Blob naming convention documented with example (yankees-roster/2026-03-31.json)
Instructions for running pytest and interpreting coverage output
Architecture overview (diagram or ASCII) showing Timer Trigger → TRAPI → Validator → Blob Storage flow
README reviewed and approved by rciapala
risks: TRAPI auth instructions depend on DES-01 resolution; may need two paths (Managed Identity vs. Key Vault credential)
